action.reasoning: Observation: Train failed immediately on make_vec_env with TypeError for unexpected 'start_method' kwarg (mp.set_start_method was set but not passed via vec_env_kwargs). Eval from iter1 showed success_rate=0.0 across all tasks with mean_rewards far below success_bars (e.g. walk=6.8<<700, package~-6173<<1500); some tasks like pole/reach had positive rewards. Pipeline (predict.py, per-task ckpts, deterministic predict, 5-tuple gym API, no bad Monitor) is correct. Analysis: Bug was in SB3 make_vec_env call; SubprocVecEnv + EGL can corrupt without proper spawn but DummyVecEnv is safer/simpler for this setup (avoids fork issues). Insufficient steps + no obs norm + small policy for humanoids. Decision: Fix to use DummyVecEnv (no start_method), keep n_envs=8 + 200k steps/task for ~10x more training than iter1, add VecNormalize (save .pkl too), update evaluate to respect max_episode_steps from config, log mean_reward per task to metrics.jsonl. This should yield positive progress on easier tasks (stand/pole/reach); submit_eval to get new success_rate and measure full iter time (~30-60min expected).
